Enacted results may not be meaningful to a first-time user
production.finalState: Dashboard loaded; Year 2020 / Turn 1 of 5 / ENACTED RESULTS; National situation dashboard; national score 66.6; adjustment cards visible.

tasks.finding: Production loads successfully and the dashboard starting point is identifiable. No preview comparison possible.

production.scrolling: Scenario presets visible after opening Immigration section.

production.finalState: Immigration Scenario Lab active year 2026; scenario No New Immigration selected.

tasks.finding: Scenario selection works on production. No preview comparison possible.

production.scrolling: Substantial task-relevant scrolling/exploration needed to reach the Assumption Ledger below scenario controls, annual result, winners/losers, myth tester and cause map.

production.finalState: No New Immigration; active year 2026; Net overseas migration changed from -80,000 people/year to 310,000 people/year; Migrant working-age share changed from 55% to 71%.

tasks.finding: Two assumptions can be changed, but the task requires enough discovery friction to mark as partial pass.

tasks.finding: One turn completes in the Immigration Lab, but cross-page year context is confusing.

production.finalState: Clicked Step Forward four additional times; Immigration Lab active year 2031, five years after starting year 2026; scenario No New Immigration.

tasks.finding: Production reaches a clear five-year result in the Immigration Lab. No preview comparison possible.

tasks.taskName: Identify the main positive and negative outcomes

production.scrolling: None beyond current visible result area.
unclearLabels: The interface does not explicitly rank most significant positive and negative outcomes
production.finalState: At 2031, visible outcomes included Population 29,420,000; Housing Stress 41; Labour Supply 51; Services Pressure 100; Environment 59. Positive identified: Labour Supply 51 / 14,121,600 working-age people. Negative identified: Services Pressure 100; Environment 59 also negative because lower is better.

tasks.finding: Positive and negative outcomes can be identified, but importance must be inferred.

production.scrolling: None beyond current visible winners/losers area.
unclearLabels: Winners/losers cards are dense and numerous
production.finalState: Winner visible: First-home buyers, POSITIVE, current 8.4, cumulative 20.1, confidence medium. Loser visible: Homeowners, NEGATIVE, current -9.4, cumulative -22.4, confidence low. Additional loser: State governments, NEGATIVE, current -16.1, cumulative -38.5.

tasks.finding: Winners and losers are available in production. No preview comparison possible.

unclearLabels: Reset scenario may be ambiguous: it resets the selected scenario, not necessarily the whole app to Current Path
production.finalState: Clicked Reset scenario; active year returned to 2026; scenario remained No New Immigration; Net overseas migration returned to -80,000 people/year; Migrant working-age share returned to 55%; 2031 result no longer appeared active.

tasks.finding: Reset scenario succeeded for the selected scenario baseline. No preview comparison possible.

acceptanceTests: Select No New Immigration, change Net overseas migration and Migrant working-age share, advance to 2031, click Reset scenario, and verify active year returns to 2026 with both changed assumptions restored to selected-scenario defaults without refresh.
Advance Immigration Lab from 2026 to 2031 and verify all visible year indicators make clear whether they refer to the whole app turn loop or the immigration topic model.
Ask a first-time user to change two immigration assumptions; pass if they can find the assumption controls without opening unrelated sections or needing facilitator help.

At a five-year result, ask a first-time user to identify the largest positive and negative outcomes; pass if the interface clearly surfaces the most significant positive and negative effects without requiring inference from many cards.
finalRecommendation: Use this report as the production baseline. Do not make a merge/release decision from this run alone; provide a Vercel preview URL and rerun the same journey for a true regression comparison.
